chapter_title.ar: باب مِيرَاثِ الْغَرْقَى وَ أَصْحَابِ الْهَدْمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – Inheritance of the drowned and the ones crushed under a collapsed building

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/204)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/204)
text: قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) بِالْيَمَنِ فِي قَوْمٍ انْهَدَمَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ دَارٌ لَهُمْ فَبَقِيَ مِنْهُمْ صَبِيَّانِ أَحَدُهُمَا مَمْلُوكٌ وَ الْآخَرُ حُرٌّ فَأَسْهَمَ بَيْنَهُمَا فَخَرَجَ السَّهْمُ عَلَى أَحَدِهِمَا فَجَعَلَ الْمَالَ لَهُ وَ أَعْتَقَ الْآخَرَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> judged at Al-Yemen regarding one people upon whom a house of theirs had collapsed. So there remained two young children, one of them being a slave, the other a free one. So he<sup>asws</sup> drew lots between the two of them, so the lot came upon one of the two, so he<sup>asws</sup> made the wealth to be for him, and emancipated (liberated) the other one’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Hariz who has narrated the following: “One of the two Imam, (abu Ja‘far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said that Amir al- Mu’minin issued a judgment in Yemen about a people on whom a house had collapsed and only two children were left, of whom one was owned and the other was free. He (the Imam) by means of raffle gave the assets to the one whose name came out and set the other one free.’”
